You are resuming `plan-slice` for `M001/S01` after a crash.
The previous session completed **3 tool calls** before dying.
Use this briefing to pick up exactly where it left off. Do NOT redo completed work.

  read `C:\Users\edwar\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\gsd-pi\pkg\extensions\gsd\templates\task-plan.md`

### Commands Already Run

### Errors Before Crash

### Resume Instructions

2. Verify files listed above exist and look correct on disk
3. Continue from where the previous session left off
4. Do NOT re-read files or re-run commands that already succeeded above

---

You are executing GSD auto-mode.

## UNIT: Plan Slice S01 ("Vite + React + Tailwind Scaffold") — Milestone M001

All relevant context has been preloaded below — start working immediately without re-reading these files.

## Inlined Context (preloaded — do not re-read these files)

### Milestone Roadmap
Source: `.gsd/milestones/M001/M001-ROADMAP.md`

# M001: Launch-Ready Oriflect Website

**Vision:** Convert completed HTML mockups into a navigable, mobile-responsive React + Vite site deployable to Vercel and Lovable, with ROI calculator and client portal concept added.

## Success Criteria

- All 7 pages render with correct Axiom Navy design system
- React Router navigation works with no full-page reloads
- Active nav state highlights current page
- Mobile hamburger menu opens/closes correctly
- Contact form shows success state on submit
- ROI calculator computes and displays results
- `npm run build` produces clean dist/ with no errors

## Key Risks / Unknowns

- Tailwind token extraction from CDN config — hyphens in color names must survive
- lh3.googleusercontent.com image URLs may 403 in some environments

## Proof Strategy

- Token extraction risk → retire in S01 by running `npm run dev` and visually confirming colors render
- Image URLs → use as-is; note in UAT for user to replace with real assets

## Verification Classes

- Contract verification: `npm run build` passes, all routes render without 404
- Integration verification: React Router `<Link>` navigation tested across all pages
- UAT / human verification: visual design check against mockup screenshots

## Milestone Definition of Done

- All 7 pages navigable via React Router
- Shared Nav + Footer on every page
- Active nav state correct per page
- Mobile responsive hamburger works
- Contact form and newsletter show feedback states
- ROI calculator functional
- `npm run build` clean
- vercel.json present for SPA routing

## Requirement Coverage

- Covers: R001, R002, R003, R004, R005, R006, R007, R008, R009, R010, R011
- Out of scope: R012, R013, R014

## Slices

- [ ] **S01: Vite + React + Tailwind Scaffold** `risk:high` `depends:[]`
  > After this: `npm run dev` serves a blank Oriflect shell at localhost:5173 with all design tokens loaded and fonts rendering

- [ ] **S02: Shared Nav + Footer Components** `risk:medium` `depends:[S01]`
  > After this: Nav and Footer render on a test page; nav links highlight active state; mobile hamburger opens/closes

- [ ] **S03: Home + Services Pages** `risk:medium` `depends:[S02]`
  > After this: Home and Services pages fully rendered from mockup content with working nav

- [ ] **S04: About + Blog + Contact Pages** `risk:medium` `depends:[S02]`
  > After this: All 5 core pages navigable; contact form and newsletter show success feedback

- [ ] **S05: Mobile Polish + Design System Pass** `risk:low` `depends:[S03,S04]`
  > After this: Site is fully responsive at all breakpoints; design system consistent with DESIGN.md spec

- [ ] **S06: ROI Calculator + Client Portal Page** `risk:medium` `depends:[S04]`
  > After this: /roi page has working calculator with real computation; /portal renders concept dashboard

## Boundary Map

### S01 → S02
Produces:
- Vite + React project at project root
- `tailwind.config.js` with full Axiom Navy token set
- `src/main.jsx` entry point
- `src/App.jsx` with React Router setup (placeholder routes)
- `src/index.css` with base styles + font imports

Consumes:
- nothing (first slice)

### S01 → S03, S04, S05, S06
Produces:
- All of the above

### S02 → S03, S04, S05, S06
Produces:
- `src/components/Nav.jsx` — sticky nav with active state via useLocation
- `src/components/Footer.jsx` — shared footer
- `src/components/Layout.jsx` — wraps Nav + children + Footer

Consumes from S01:
- Tailwind config tokens
- React Router context

### S03 → S05
Produces:
- `src/pages/Home.jsx`
- `src/pages/Services.jsx`

### S04 → S05, S06
Produces:
- `src/pages/About.jsx`
- `src/pages/Blog.jsx`
- `src/pages/Contact.jsx` with form state
- Newsletter subscribe state in Blog.jsx

### S05 → S06
Produces:
- Responsive polish applied to all pages
- No new interfaces, just quality

### S06 (terminal)
Produces:
- `src/pages/ROICalculator.jsx` — stateful calculator
- `src/pages/ClientPortal.jsx` — concept dashboard
- Routes /roi and /portal registered in App.jsx

---

### Slice Research
Source: `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S01/S01-RESEARCH.md`

# S01 Research: Vite + React + Tailwind Scaffold

**Gathered:** 2026-03-25
**Slice:** S01 — Vite + React + Tailwind Scaffold
**Status:** Complete (scaffold already exists and is functional)

## Summary

The Vite + React + Tailwind scaffold was already created prior to this research pass. All S01 deliverables exist and `npm run build` passes clean. S01 is effectively done — the planning phase should confirm this and mark it complete rather than re-scaffolding.

## What Exists

### Project root
`oriflect/` — the Vite project lives as a subdirectory of the workspace root, not at project root. This is a mild deviation from the S01 spec (which assumed project root), but is reasonable. S02 onward must target `oriflect/src/` not `src/`.

### Installed stack
- `vite@8.0.1` + `@vitejs/plugin-react@6.0.1`
- `react@19.2.4` + `react-dom@19.2.4`
- `react-router-dom@7.13.2` (note: v7, not v6 — API is compatible for our use; `<Routes>`, `<Route>`, `useLocation`, `<Link>` all work identically)
- `tailwindcss@3.4.19` + `postcss@8.5.8` + `autoprefixer`

### Config files present
| File | Status |
|------|--------|
| `oriflect/tailwind.config.js` | ✅ Full Axiom Navy token set extracted |
| `oriflect/postcss.config.js` | ✅ Present |
| `oriflect/vite.config.js` | ✅ Present |
| `oriflect/vercel.json` | ✅ SPA rewrite rule present |
| `oriflect/index.html` | ✅ Space Grotesk + Inter + Material Symbols imported from Google Fonts |

### Source files present
| File | Status |
|------|--------|
| `src/main.jsx` | ✅ BrowserRouter wrapping App |
| `src/App.jsx` | ✅ All 7 routes registered under Layout |
| `src/index.css` | ✅ Tailwind directives + base styles + grid-bg/glass-header utilities |
| `src/components/Nav.jsx` | ✅ Exists (S02 work already started) |
| `src/components/Footer.jsx` | ✅ Exists |
| `src/components/Layout.jsx` | ✅ Exists |
| `src/pages/Home.jsx` | ✅ Exists |
| `src/pages/Services.jsx` | ✅ Exists |
| `src/pages/About.jsx` | ✅ Exists |
| `src/pages/Blog.jsx` | ✅ Exists |
| `src/pages/Contact.jsx` | ✅ Exists |
| `src/pages/ROICalculator.jsx` | ✅ Exists |
| `src/pages/ClientPortal.jsx` | ✅ Exists |

### Build verification
```
npm run build → ✓ built in 391ms
dist/index.html                   1.14 kB
dist/assets/index-*.css          28.15 kB
dist/assets/index-*.js          308.21 kB
```
Zero errors, zero warnings.

## Design Token Status

All Axiom Navy tokens are present in `tailwind.config.js`:
- Full surface hierarchy: `surface`, `surface-container`, `surface-container-low`, `surface-container-high`, `surface-container-highest`, `surface-container-lowest`, `surface-bright`, `surface-dim`
- Primary gold: `primary` (#e9c176), `primary-fixed`, `primary-fixed-dim`, `primary-dark` (#D4AF37)
- Secondary / tertiary palettes complete
- Font families: `headline` (Space Grotesk), `body` (Inter), `label` (Space Grotesk)
- Border radius: all variants zeroed out (0px), `full` kept at 9999px — matches Axiom Navy 0px radius spec

Custom utilities in `index.css`:
- `.grid-bg` — dot-matrix radial gradient
- `.ghost-grid` — secondary dot grid
- `.ghost-border` — muted outline border
- `.hero-gradient` — bottom-fade for hero sections
- `.glass-header` — backdrop-blur nav panel

## Key Findings / Risks

### React Router v7 (not v6)
`react-router-dom@7.13.2` is installed. The S01 plan specified v6. In practice, the public API used here (`<BrowserRouter>`, `<Routes>`, `<Route>`, `<Link>`, `useLocation`, `useNavigate`) is identical between v6 and v7 for our use case. No action needed.

### Project in subdirectory
The Vite project lives at `oriflect/` not at the workspace root. This is fine for `npm run dev` and Vercel deploys (Vercel root directory can be set to `oriflect`). All subsequent slice work should be done inside `oriflect/`.

### Font + icon loading
Google Fonts and Material Symbols loaded via CDN `<link>` in `index.html`. This works in dev and production. No offline concern for this project scope.

### Image URLs
`lh3.googleusercontent.com/aida-public/` URLs are used in mockups. Risk noted in M001-CONTEXT is real — these may 403 in production. `src/assets/hero.png` exists as a local asset. Pages should prefer local assets where possible; document the substitution need.

### Lovable compatibility
Standard Vite + React + Tailwind structure. React 19 is newer than Lovable's typical tested range (React 18). If Lovable import fails, downgrading to react@18 is the lever. Note for UAT.

## Requirements Coverage

| Req | Status |
|-----|--------|
| R004 — Axiom Navy design system | ✅ Tokens extracted, fonts loaded, utilities defined |
| R008 — Deployable to Vercel + importable to Lovable | ✅ vercel.json present, build clean, standard structure |

## Skills Assessment

- No additional skills needed for S01. Frontend-design skill is available if needed for page rendering in later slices.
- `npx skills find "vite react tailwind"` not needed — standard tools, docs available, scaffold complete.

## Forward Intelligence for S02+

- All components and pages already exist as files. S02 work is filling in Nav/Footer/Layout content from mockups, not creating them from scratch.
- The `dark` class is set on `<html>` — Tailwind dark mode is class-based. If any component uses `dark:` variants, they will activate globally. Keep this in mind when porting mockup styles.
- `react-router-dom` v7 exports are from `react-router-dom` (not `react-router`) — import paths are the same as v6.
- `npm run dev` must be run from `oriflect/` directory, not the workspace root.

---

### Decisions
Source: `.gsd/DECISIONS.md`

# Decisions Register

<!-- Append-only. Never edit or remove existing rows. -->

| # | When | Scope | Decision | Choice | Rationale | Revisable? |
|---|------|-------|----------|--------|-----------|------------|
| D001 | M001 | arch | Frontend framework | React + Vite | Lovable runs React natively; Vercel deploys Vite out of the box; no build complexity | No |
| D002 | M001 | library | CSS framework | Tailwind CSS via npm | Extracted from CDN config in mockups; needed for tree-shaking in production build | No |
| D003 | M001 | library | Routing | React Router v6 | Standard for React SPAs; Vercel SPA routing via vercel.json | No |
| D004 | M001 | convention | No TypeScript | Plain JS (.jsx) | Simpler for Lovable import and visual editing; user did not request TS | Yes — if Lovable supports TS |
| D005 | M001 | arch | Form handling | Client-side only with simulated success state | No backend in scope; forms show feedback without submission | Yes — when backend added |
| D006 | M001 | convention | Images | Keep lh3.googleusercontent.com URLs from mockups | Fastest path to launch; user can replace with real assets later | Yes — production asset replacement |

---

### Requirements
Source: `.gsd/REQUIREMENTS.md`

# Requirements

## Active

### R001 — 5-page site with working React Router navigation
- Class: primary-user-loop
- Status: active
- Description: Home, Services, About, Blog, Contact all navigable via React Router with no full-page reloads
- Why it matters: Core product — without navigation the site doesn't function
- Source: user
- Primary owning slice: M001/S03
- Supporting slices: M001/S04
- Validation: unmapped

### R002 — Shared Nav with active-state highlighting
- Class: primary-user-loop
- Status: active
- Description: Navigation component shows active page via gold underline/color; present on every page
- Why it matters: Orientation and brand consistency
- Source: user
- Primary owning slice: M001/S02
- Supporting slices: none
- Validation: unmapped

### R003 — Shared Footer consistent across all pages
- Class: primary-user-loop
- Status: active
- Description: Footer with ORIFLECT logo, nav links, copyright renders on every page
- Why it matters: Brand closure on every page
- Source: user
- Primary owning slice: M001/S02
- Supporting slices: none
- Validation: unmapped

### R004 — Axiom Navy design system applied faithfully
- Class: quality-attribute
- Status: active
- Description: 0px border radius throughout, surface hierarchy (surface/surface-container-low/etc.), muted gold primary, ghost borders, dot-matrix grid backgrounds, Space Grotesk + Inter fonts
- Why it matters: Differentiating brand — if the design regresses to generic it undermines the product
- Source: user
- Primary owning slice: M001/S01
- Supporting slices: M001/S02, M001/S03, M001/S04, M001/S05
- Validation: unmapped

### R005 — Mobile-responsive hamburger nav
- Class: launchability
- Status: active
- Description: Navigation collapses to hamburger on mobile; menu opens/closes; all links work
- Why it matters: Non-negotiable for launch — majority of visits will be mobile
- Source: inferred
- Primary owning slice: M001/S05
- Supporting slices: none
- Validation: unmapped

### R006 — Contact form with visible feedback state
- Class: primary-user-loop
- Status: active
- Description: Contact form shows success message on submit (client-side); required field validation
- Why it matters: Primary conversion action — broken form loses leads
- Source: inferred
- Primary owning slice: M001/S04
- Supporting slices: none
- Validation: unmapped

### R007 — Blog newsletter subscribe with feedback state
- Class: primary-user-loop
- Status: active
- Description: Newsletter email input shows success/error feedback on submit
- Why it matters: Secondary conversion action for the blog
- Source: inferred
- Primary owning slice: M001/S04
- Supporting slices: none
- Validation: unmapped

### R008 — Deployable to Vercel + importable to Lovable
- Class: launchability
- Status: active
- Description: React + Vite + Tailwind project structure; npm run dev starts; npm run build produces dist/; standard enough for Lovable import
- Why it matters: User's explicit launch target
- Source: user
- Primary owning slice: M001/S01
- Supporting slices: none
- Validation: unmapped

### R009 — About page founder story content
- Class: core-capability
- Status: active
- Description: About page renders Edward Griggs founder story, mission statement, core values — Phase 3 content refinement
- Why it matters: Trust-building page — central to lead conversion
- Source: user
- Primary owning slice: M001/S04
- Supporting slices: none
- Validation: unmapped

### R010 — Interactive ROI Calculator
- Class: differentiator
- Status: active
- Description: /roi page with inputs (employees, hours saved, hourly rate) that compute projected annual savings and payback period
- Why it matters: Converts skeptical SMB owners by making the value proposition concrete and personal
- Source: user
- Primary owning slice: M001/S06
- Supporting slices: none
- Validation: unmapped

### R011 — Client Portal concept page
- Class: differentiator
- Status: active
- Description: /portal page showing a concept dashboard UI — project status, deliverables, communication log
- Why it matters: Signals professionalism and positions Oriflect as a long-term partner
- Source: user
- Primary owning slice: M001/S06
- Supporting slices: none
- Validation: unmapped

## Out of Scope

### R012 — Calendly live integration
- Class: integration
- Status: out-of-scope

- Notes: Placeholder present, live embed deferred

### R013 — Backend / form submission server
- Class: integration

- Description: Forms are client-side only with simulated success state

### R014 — CMS integration
- Class: integration

- Description: Blog posts are static; no headless CMS

## Traceability

| ID | Class | Status | Primary owner | Proof |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| R001 | primary-user-loop | active | M001/S03 | unmapped |
| R002 | primary-user-loop | active | M001/S02 | unmapped |
| R003 | primary-user-loop | active | M001/S02 | unmapped |
| R004 | quality-attribute | active | M001/S01 | unmapped |
| R005 | launchability | active | M001/S05 | unmapped |
| R006 | primary-user-loop | active | M001/S04 | unmapped |
| R007 | primary-user-loop | active | M001/S04 | unmapped |
| R008 | launchability | active | M001/S01 | unmapped |
| R009 | core-capability | active | M001/S04 | unmapped |
| R010 | differentiator | active | M001/S06 | unmapped |

### Dependency Slice Summaries

Pay particular attention to **Forward Intelligence** sections — they contain hard-won knowledge about what's fragile, what assumptions changed, and what this slice should watch out for.

- (no dependencies)

Narrate your decomposition reasoning — why you're grouping work this way, what risks are driving the order, what verification strategy you're choosing and why.

Then:
0. If `REQUIREMENTS.md` was preloaded above, identify which Active requirements the roadmap says this slice owns or supports. These are the requirements this plan must deliver — every owned requirement needs at least one task that directly advances it, and verification must prove the requirement is met.
1. Read the templates:
   - `~/.gsd/agent/extensions/gsd/templates/plan.md`
   - `~/.gsd/agent/extensions/gsd/templates/task-plan.md`
2. If a `GSD Skill Preferences` block is present in system context, use it to decide which skills to load and follow during planning, without overriding required plan formatting
3. Define slice-level verification first — the objective stopping condition for this slice:
   - For non-trivial slices: plan actual test files with real assertions. Name the files. The first task creates them (initially failing). Remaining tasks make them pass.
   - For simple slices: executable commands or script assertions are fine.
   - If the project is non-trivial and has no test framework, the first task should set one up.
   - If this slice establishes a boundary contract, verification must exercise that contract.
4. Plan observability and diagnostics explicitly:
   - For non-trivial backend, integration, async, stateful, or UI slices, include an `Observability / Diagnostics` section in the slice plan.
   - Define how a future agent will inspect state, detect failure, and localize the problem.
   - Prefer structured logs/events, stable error codes/types, status surfaces, and persisted failure state over ad hoc debug text.
   - Include at least one verification check for a diagnostic or failure-path signal when relevant.
5. Fill the `Proof Level` and `Integration Closure` sections truthfully:
   - State whether the slice proves contract, integration, operational, or final-assembly behavior.
   - Say whether real runtime or human/UAT is required.
   - Name the wiring introduced in this slice and what still remains before the milestone is truly usable end-to-end.
6. Decompose the slice into tasks, each fitting one context window
7. Every task in the slice plan should be written as an executable increment with:
   - a concrete, action-oriented title
   - the inline task entry fields defined in the plan.md template (Why / Files / Do / Verify / Done when)
   - a matching task plan containing description, steps, must-haves, verification, observability impact, inputs, and expected output
8. Each task needs: title, description, steps, must-haves, verification, observability impact, inputs, and expected output
9. If verification includes test files, ensure the first task includes creating them with expected assertions (they should fail initially — that's correct)
10. Write `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S01/S01-PLAN.md`
11. Write individual task plans in `C:\Users\edwar\Desktop\Oriflect Site\.gsd\milestones\M001\slices\S01/tasks/`: `T01-PLAN.md`, `T02-PLAN.md`, etc.
12. **Self-audit the plan before continuing.** Walk through each check — if any fail, fix the plan files before moving on:
    - **Completion semantics:** If every task were completed exactly as written, the slice goal/demo should actually be true at the claimed proof level. Do not allow a task plan that only scaffolds toward a future working state.
    - **Requirement coverage:** Every must-have in the slice maps to at least one task. No must-have is orphaned.
    - **Task completeness:** Every task has steps, must-haves, verification, observability impact, inputs, and expected output — none are blank or vague.
    - **Dependency correctness:** Task ordering is consistent. No task references work from a later task.
    - **Key links planned:** For every pair of artifacts that must connect (component → API, API → database, form → handler), there is an explicit step that wires them — not just "create X" and "create Y" in separate tasks with no connection step.
    - **Scope sanity:** Target 2–5 steps and 3–8 files per task. 6–8 steps or 8–10 files is a warning — consider splitting. 10+ steps or 12+ files — must split. Each task must be completable in a single fresh context window.
    - **Context compliance:** If context/research artifacts or `.gsd/DECISIONS.md` exist, the plan honors locked decisions and doesn't include deferred or out-of-scope items.
    - **Requirement coverage:** If `REQUIREMENTS.md` exists, every Active requirement this slice owns (per the roadmap) maps to at least one task with verification that proves the requirement is met. No owned requirement is left without a task. No task claims to satisfy a requirement that is Deferred or Out of Scope.
    - **Proof honesty:** The `Proof Level` and `Integration Closure` sections match what this slice will actually prove, and they do not imply live end-to-end completion if only fixture or contract proof is planned.
    - **Feature completeness:** Every task produces real, user-facing progress — not just internal scaffolding. If the slice has a UI surface, at least one task builds the real UI (not a placeholder). If the slice has an API, at least one task connects it to a real data source (not hardcoded returns). If every task were completed and you showed the result to a non-technical stakeholder, they should see real product progress, not developer artifacts.
13. If planning produced structural decisions (e.g. verification strategy, observability strategy, technology choices, patterns to follow), append them to `.gsd/DECISIONS.md`
14. Commit: `docs(S01): add slice plan`
15. Update `.gsd/STATE.md`

The slice directory and tasks/ subdirectory already exist. Do NOT mkdir. You are on the slice branch; all work stays here.

**You MUST write the file `C:\Users\edwar\Desktop\Oriflect Site\.gsd\milestones\M001\slices\S01\S01-PLAN.md` before finishing.**

When done, say: "Slice S01 planned."

content.thinking: Let me look at what exists and then write the plan files.

content.text:
# {{sliceId}}: {{sliceTitle}}

**Goal:** {{goal}}
**Demo:** {{demo}}

## Must-Haves

- {{mustHave}}
- {{mustHave}}

## Proof Level

- This slice proves: {{contract | integration | operational | final-assembly}}
- Real runtime required: {{yes/no}}
- Human/UAT required: {{yes/no}}

## Verification

<!-- Define what "done" looks like BEFORE detailing tasks.
     This section is the slice's objective stopping condition — execution isn't done
     until everything here passes.

     For non-trivial projects:
     - Write actual test files into the codebase during the first task
     - Tests should assert on the slice's demo outcome and boundary contracts
     - Name the test files here so execution has an unambiguous target

     For simple projects or scripts:
     - Executable verification commands (bash assertions, curl checks, etc.) are sufficient

     If the project has no test framework and the work is non-trivial,
     the first task should set one up. A test runner costs 2 minutes
     and pays for itself immediately.

     For non-trivial backend, integration, async, stateful, or UI work:
     - Include at least one verification check for an observability or failure-path signal
     - Verify not just that the feature works, but that a future agent can inspect its state when it fails -->

- {{testFileOrCommand — e.g. `npm test -- --grep "auth flow"` or `bash scripts/verify-s01.sh`}}
- {{testFileOrCommand}}

## Observability / Diagnostics

<!-- Required for non-trivial backend, integration, async, stateful, or UI slices.
     Describe how a future agent will inspect current state, detect failure,
     and localize the problem with minimal ambiguity.

     Prefer:
     - structured logs/events over ad hoc console strings
     - stable error codes/types over vague failures
     - health/readiness/status surfaces over hidden internal state
     - persisted failure state when it materially improves retries or recovery

     Keep this section concise and high-signal. Do not log secrets or sensitive raw payloads. -->

- Runtime signals: {{structured log/event, state transition, metric, or none}}
- Inspection surfaces: {{status endpoint, CLI command, script, UI state, DB table, or none}}
- Failure visibility: {{last error, retry count, phase, timestamp, correlation id, or none}}
- Redaction constraints: {{secret/PII boundary or none}}

## Integration Closure

- Upstream surfaces consumed: {{specific files / modules / contracts}}
- New wiring introduced in this slice: {{entrypoint / composition / runtime hookup, or none}}
- What remains before the milestone is truly usable end-to-end: {{list or "nothing"}}

## Tasks

<!--
  If every task below is completed exactly as written, the Goal and Demo above
  should be true at the stated proof level. Tasks should close the loop on the
  slice, not merely prepare for later work unless the Demo truthfully says the
  slice only proves fixture/contract-level behavior.

  Write each task as an executable increment, not a vague intention.

  Prefer action-oriented titles:
  - "Wire real auth middleware into dashboard routes"
  - "Persist job status and expose failure diagnostics"
  - "Add browser test covering empty-state recovery"

  Avoid vague titles:
  - "Set up auth"
  - "Handle errors"
  - "Improve UI"

  Each task should usually include:
  - Why: why this task exists / what part of the slice it closes
  - Files: the main files likely touched
  - Do: concrete implementation steps and important constraints
  - Verify: the command, test, or runtime check that proves it worked

arguments.content:
# S01: Vite + React + Tailwind Scaffold

**Goal:** Confirm that the existing scaffold fully satisfies S01 requirements — all design tokens loaded, fonts rendering, build clean, dev server starts, and the S02 boundary contract is intact.
**Demo:** `npm run dev` from `oriflect/` serves a React shell at localhost:5173; `npm run build` produces a clean dist/; Tailwind Axiom Navy tokens are active; vercel.json present.

## Must-Haves

- Vite + React project at `oriflect/` with `npm run dev` starting without errors
- Tailwind config contains full Axiom Navy token set (surfaces, primary gold, typography, 0px radius)
- `npm run build` produces clean `dist/` with no errors or warnings
- `vercel.json` present with SPA rewrite rule
- `index.html` imports Space Grotesk + Inter + Material Symbols from Google Fonts
- React Router registered in `src/App.jsx` with all 7 routes
- `src/index.css` has Tailwind directives + custom utilities (grid-bg, glass-header, hero-gradient)

## Proof Level

- This slice proves: contract
- Real runtime required: yes (dev server + browser visual confirmation)
- Human/UAT required: no (automated build verification sufficient; visual is a bonus)

## Verification

- `cd oriflect && npm run build` exits 0 with no errors
- `cd oriflect && npm run dev` starts and serves on port 5173
- Browser at localhost:5173 renders without console errors
- `grep "axiom-navy\|surface\|primary" oriflect/tailwind.config.js | wc -l` returns > 10

## Observability / Diagnostics

- Runtime signals: Vite dev server stdout shows "ready in Xms" and port; build stdout shows file sizes
- Inspection surfaces: `oriflect/dist/` after build; browser console at localhost:5173
- Failure visibility: Vite build errors surface to stdout with file + line; missing tokens show as unstyled elements
- Redaction constraints: none

## Integration Closure

- Upstream surfaces consumed: none (first slice)
- New wiring introduced in this slice: Vite + React + Tailwind project at `oriflect/`; React Router wrapping all 7 routes; Tailwind design token config; vercel.json SPA rewrite
- What remains before the milestone is truly usable end-to-end: Nav/Footer/Layout components with real content (S02); page content from mockups (S03, S04); mobile polish (S05); ROI calculator + portal (S06)

## Tasks

- [x] **T01: Verify scaffold integrity and confirm S02 boundary contract** `est:20m`
  - Why: Research confirmed the scaffold exists and builds. This task runs the verifications, checks the boundary outputs S02 depends on, and marks S01 complete.
  - Files: `oriflect/tailwind.config.js`, `oriflect/vercel.json`, `oriflect/index.html`, `oriflect/src/App.jsx`, `oriflect/src/index.css`, `oriflect/src/main.jsx`
  - Do: Run `npm run build` from `oriflect/`; start dev server and confirm port 5173 responds; check tailwind config has surface/primary token keys; confirm vercel.json has rewrite rule; confirm App.jsx has all 7 routes; confirm index.css has @tailwind directives and custom utilities
  - Verify: `npm run build` exits 0; localhost:5173 serves without 404 or console errors; grep confirms token presence
  - Done when: All verification checks above pass; S01 marked complete in roadmap

## Files Likely Touched

- `oriflect/tailwind.config.js`
- `oriflect/vercel.json`
- `oriflect/index.html`
- `oriflect/src/App.jsx`
- `oriflect/src/main.jsx`
- `oriflect/src/index.css`

---
estimated_steps: 5
estimated_files: 6
---

# T01: Verify scaffold integrity and confirm S02 boundary contract

**Slice:** S01 — Vite + React + Tailwind Scaffold
**Milestone:** M001

## Description

The scaffold was built before formal GSD tracking began. Research confirmed all files exist and `npm run build` passes. This task runs the objective verifications, checks every S02 boundary output, and marks S01 complete. No code changes expected — this is a confirmation pass.

## Steps

1. Run `cd oriflect && npm run build` — confirm exit 0 and no error lines in output
2. Start dev server (`npm run dev`) and confirm Vite reports ready on port 5173
3. Check `tailwind.config.js` contains surface hierarchy tokens and primary gold tokens
4. Check `vercel.json` contains a rewrite rule routing `/*` to `/index.html`
5. Check `src/App.jsx` has all 7 routes registered and React Router `<BrowserRouter>` or `<RouterProvider>` wrapping; check `src/index.css` has `@tailwind` directives + `.grid-bg`, `.glass-header` utilities

## Must-Haves

- [ ] `npm run build` exits 0 with zero errors and zero warnings
- [ ] Dev server starts on port 5173 without error
- [ ] `tailwind.config.js` contains `surface`, `primary`, `surface-container` token keys
- [ ] `vercel.json` has SPA rewrite rule present
- [ ] `src/App.jsx` registers all 7 routes (home, services, about, blog, contact, roi, portal)
- [ ] `src/index.css` has `@tailwind base`, `@tailwind components`, `@tailwind utilities` and `.grid-bg` / `.glass-header` class definitions

## Verification

- `cd oriflect && npm run build` — stdout contains file sizes, no "error" lines
- `grep -c "surface\|primary" oriflect/tailwind.config.js` returns ≥ 10
- `grep "rewrites\|destination" oriflect/vercel.json` returns matches
- `grep -c "path=" oriflect/src/App.jsx` returns ≥ 7

## Observability Impact

- Signals added/changed: None — pure verification task
- How a future agent inspects this: Run `npm run build` from `oriflect/`; errors surface to stdout with file + line
- Failure state exposed: Build failures are explicit; token absence visible via grep

## Inputs

- `oriflect/` — existing scaffold from pre-GSD work
- `S01-RESEARCH.md` — confirms what exists and what to check

## Expected Output

- All verification checks pass
- S01 marked `[x]` in `M001-ROADMAP.md`
- `S01-PLAN.md` task T01 marked `[x]`
- `STATE.md` updated to reflect S01 complete, S02 next
